hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: How Much, How Many -- Talking About Quantities and Data with Global
lesson.menu_desc: A Global quer os números do Brasil: quantos pacientes, quanta cobertura, quantos hospitais. A aula dos QUANTIFIERS -- much, many, a lot of, a few, a little -- para descrever quantidades e dados de campo em voz alta, num field update com o time Global. How MANY patients? How MUCH access? -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 19 -- How Much, How Many (Quantifiers)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Lesson 19 -- How Much, How Many (Quantifiers)
lesson.grammar_point: quantifiers (much, many, a lot of, a few, a little)

listenings.text: Hi Emmanuele, it's David from the Global team. A quick brief before your field update on Thursday. We want the numbers, but keep them simple. First, how many patients are on the program right now? An average is fine. Second, how much access do we have in the public market, and how much in the private market? A few clear figures are better than a lot of detail. And third, how many hospitals are ready for the launch? If only a few are ready, that is important to know. Do not worry about perfect data. We want the big picture. See you Thursday.

Hi Emmanuele, it's Claire from the Global data team. I've looked at the Brazil dashboard, and I have a few comments. The majority of your patients are in the public system, which is great. But a large portion of the private market still has very little coverage. We have a lot of demand, but not many hospitals are ready yet. So my advice is simple. For Thursday, show the ratio of public to private, give one average for patient volume, and name a few priorities. That is all the Global team needs. Thank you, and good luck.

extra_audio.text: Hi David, here is a quick update on the numbers. We have many patients on the program. A lot of demand is in the private market. But we have only a few hospitals ready for the launch. And we still have a little time before the deadline.

items.situation: David asks: "How many patients are on the program?"
tips: We have many patients on the program.
Approximately two hundred patients, on average.

items.situation: "How much access do we have in the private market?"
tips: We have a lot of demand, but a little access.
Only a small portion of the private market has coverage.

items.situation: "How many hospitals are ready for the launch?"
tips: Only a few hospitals are ready.
A few are ready. The majority are still not.

items.situation: "Is there much demand in the public system?"
tips: Yes, there is a lot of demand.
The majority of our patients are in the public system.

items.situation: "How much time do we have before the deadline?"
tips: We have a little time before Q3.
Not much time. A few weeks.

items.situation: "What is the ratio of public to private patients?"
tips: The ratio is about three to one.
The majority are public, a minority are private.
